sn35.105:1.1: "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, do pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn35.105:1.2: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
sn35.105:1.3: "When there is the eye, bhikkhus, by clinging to the eye, pleasure and pain arise internally. When there is the ear … the mind, by clinging to the mind, pleasure and pain arise internally.
sn35.105:1.4: "What do you think, bhikkhus, is the eye permanent or impermanent?"
sn35.105:1.6: "Is what is impermanent suffering or happiness?"
sn35.105:1.8: "But without clinging to what is impermanent, suffering, and subject to change, could pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn35.105:2.2: "Is the ear … the mind permanent or impermanent? … But without clinging to what is impermanent, suffering, and subject to change, could pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn35.105:3.3:
"Seeing thus, bhikkhus, the instructed noble disciple experiences revulsion towards the eye … the mind. Experiencing revulsion, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion
is liberated. When it is liberated there comes the knowledge: ‘It's liberated.’ He understands: ‘Destroyed is birth, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more for this state of being.’"
